We have a new thing and I ignore a lot of gossip and I ignore a lot of criticism so you kind of have to pick is this significant or is this not significant but in aggressively reacting to we have a thing surfaced and I don't haunt the internet but obviously there are people who haunt the internet and have no lives and so there are like Kennedy chat rooms you know where people just go to talk about me and by the way you may have them and aren't aware of them or you may be aware of them but so we very recently have had its surface that Dan doesn't even write the newsletter anymore well see that like is significant so there's a lot of shit that can be ignored and should be ignored but that one can't be ignored so that one now has to be aggressively dealt with because that can't be allowed to get legs hard to undo them when they gain speed you know and so you'll see some of it some of it you won't see but I mean you'll see a pretty aggressive response to that and it will color not only will there be aggressive response but now there will be deliberate commentary in newsletters to clarify who's writing what so now it moves over onto my little checklist you know that somehow we allowed a room for that to happen that I have to tighten up that space and I have to directly address it so there's a principle of prevention essentially there's what do you want to be known for and what do you make sure what do you want to make sure you're not known for and how do you address that in all of your writing and all of your communications and Dan do you address it will you aggressively do that to your customers or do you bother going back both in some cases certainly aggressively to your customers but in some cases yeah you got to go kick the originator of the stuff in the nuts but but it is of greater importance to address it than it is to go kick the moron in the nuts you know you talked about exaggerating the story and you gave examples like Dr. Laura who wasn't actually a doctor so in this day and age you're considering she made up a persona she wasn't actually a doctor someone jumps on an internet forum and says she's not a doctor how do you handle this well the principle of prevention tells you that you that you have a number of choices to start with first of all there was no benefit to her for that positioning to start with it wasn't necessary see that's this confusion of resume versus story right see the public doesn't care she's not a doctor they only care if they find out about it after the fact and she presented herself as something she couldn't support and now she does have a doctorate as you know but it's from you know it's from the place where you fill out four pages and get two grand and you've got a doctorate there's a lot of those floating around by the way there's a couple famous people in our world I won't mention them who got their doctorates from the same place that she got her doctorate by the way Columbia Pacific University which was a post office box created by a guy by the name of John Bearer that issues doctors doctorates a very profitable business for a number of years there's a whole lot of people who want to have them without doing the work but see in reality the public don't care about that right unless you've you've conned them and now it explodes all over you so why do it in the first place I agree with you that the internet has made this it's made it far more sensitive than it used to be because there's a way for it to spread fast you know that that didn't exist before and so we're going to get to it I have a thing I call one of the principles here is what I call the principle of transparency I think you're better off see she didn't embellish her story she faked her story and there's a difference and it's a distinct difference and when you get your hand caught in that cookie jar you know it's like Gore's problems with the I inspired love story written by my roommate and then the guy says you had nothing to do with that story I invented the internet well you didn't invent the internet see that's stupid shit because you can't support it it wasn't necessary you know it wasn't essential to you I don't even know if it was helpful to your story line so why put yourself in that position to start with and to stay on this part of the principle of prevention again is to think about see how we said there was a time when I thought about running for a political office and I had made a little list of all of the things that had to be in my announcement speech that were disclosures and you know she's the announcement speech is three hours long you know because I got a lot of dysfunctional relatives living in dead and they've been in jail you know they've chased each other in the street with guns they deal drugs they got DUIs well you know you got to dump all of it because it's going to come out anyway and then there's my list of shit and jeez by the time you get done you know the campaign's over and you're still giving the disclosure so that was a discouraging you know factor but I think that's the way to play the game I think the way to play the game is with embellished reality not with lies and disguises and hides and see like it's my contention Laura would have been everybody's popular admitting from the very beginning see because you could sell against that I'm not an egghead psychologist who sits in an office all day and you know works out of a textbook I'm coming to you from real experience I see so it's completely unnecessary and consistently people get themselves nailed over these fabrications you know and in most cases they weren't particularly helpful to them to start with okay principle six on 67 watching the clock is the principle of mental imprint another good book to read is orange cover it's the idea that sticks made it made to stick yeah interesting about those guys by the way I actually made an attempt to get them here they have no way to be communicated with you can't like get into them is like a horror show no no no I don't but but the content of their stuff they did a really good research job they did a really good research job and there's great parallels to what we've already been talking about but the the oh I wouldn't do this I tried I was ready to give money and you can't you know they don't have a way to take money kind of George Ross's thing in the Burger King in the apprentice episode you know you guys don't have a way to take the money six cash registers one of them is open not good so mental imprint is about what will stick the most right and and really this gets us to story because more than anything else what will stick with people is the story not the teaching not the core content not the seven principles of this story but the story and so that suggests taking great care because the story will stick kind of whether you want it to stick or not and that's the way to imprint in people's minds what you're about how they should connect with you how they should think about you is through a story the you guys have all seen the war the roses movie right the old divorce movie with Michael Douglas and Danny David Olsner great movie so if you don't remember the premise of the movie is this little nerdy guy comes into David O's divorce
